description: A near-future high-tech low-life cyberpunk d20 lightweight rule; emphasizes cyberware, hacking, corporate oppression, gang conflict, heat tracking, and street survival; does not correspond to Cyberpunk 2020/RED official rules.

special_stats.name: Humanity
special_stats.description: How much the character retains of self, emotion, and social bonds. Trauma, betrayal, extreme cyberization, or corporate brainwashing lower humanity.

special_stats.name: Cyberware Load
special_stats.description: The burden implanted cyberware places on the body and nervous system. The closer to the limit, the more rejection, maintenance cost, humanity loss, or hacking risk.

special_stats.name: Heat
special_stats.description: How much corporate security, police, gangs, or bounty hunters are watching the character. Higher heat means more frequent checks, tracking, and retaliation.

attr_hint: Attributes use 3-18 values; 10 is roughly average. This rule keeps the d20 six-attribute skeleton: Strength leans toward melee and carry capacity, Dexterity toward firearms/stealth/driving, Constitution toward damage soak and cyberware tolerance, Intelligence toward hacking/tech/investigation, Wisdom toward scouting/reading situations, Charisma toward streetwise/deals/intimidation.
skill_hint: Skills represent street expertise, professional training, or black-market experience. At creation pick 3-4 core skills with values 20-80; 40 is reliable entry-level, 60 is professional, 80 is a signature talent. Hacking, cyberware, corporate etiquette, and street relationships should carry costs in the story.

classes.name: Street Merc
classes.description: Frontline operator who makes a living with guns, cyberware, and guts.

classes.name: Hacker
classes.description: A netghost who intrudes on systems, bypasses ICE, steals data, and sows electronic chaos.

classes.name: Tech
classes.description: Expert at repairing cyberware, modding weapons, operating drones, and dismantling security systems.

classes.name: Fixer
classes.description: Broker who connects corps, gangs, and the black market, negotiating prices and selling intel.

classes.name: Corp
classes.description: Insider renegade or agent who knows corporate process, contract traps, and office warfare.

classes.name: Nomad Driver
classes.description: Driver, smuggler, and route expert surviving in the wasteland outside the city and the logistics underworld.

classes.name: Media
classes.description: Tracks truth, agitates public opinion, and exposes dirt; may also be devoured by the truth.

classes.name: Street Doc
classes.description: Underground doctor in back-alley clinics stitching gunshots, implanting cyberware, and handling illegal pharma.

gm_prompt_appendix:
You are the GM of a near-future cyberpunk world. Megacorporations rule infrastructure, media, healthcare, security, and law; gangs control the districts and the black market fills every gap; players survive between neon, acid rain, slums, and steel skyscrapers.

This is a d20 lightweight rule, not Cyberpunk 2020/RED. Put the cyberpunk flavor into narrative structure and cost:
- Each job must involve at least one of: corp, gang, police, black market, media, or fixers; never let a commission look clean and harmless.
- Information matters more than firepower. Encourage reconnaissance, negotiation, hacking, infiltration, identity spoofing, framing scapegoats, and timely retreat.
- Hackers are not master keys. Intrusion faces ICE, firewalls, tracing, alarms, honeypots, air gaps, or counter-hackers; failure raises heat or exposes identity.
- Cyberware is powerful but costly. New cyberware may add cyberware load, maintenance fees, hacker backdoors, rejection, humanity loss, corp warranty locks, or debt.
- Combat should be dangerous, brief, and expensive. Heavy fire draws security, drones, media, or gang retaliation; gunshots always have consequences.
- Heat represents how much attention is on the party. Open killing, failed hacks, left evidence, offending a corp, or betraying a gang should raise heat; lying low, framing others, clearing evidence, or deals can lower it.
- Humanity represents how much self the character retains. Cruel choices, betraying friends, over-cyberization, trauma, and corporate brainwashing lower humanity; relationships, rest, treatment, and holding the line can restore a little.

Resource tags: when needed, use STATE or narrative tags to describe changes to cyberware load, humanity, and heat; do not over-tally, but major costs must land on the character sheet or as story consequences.

difficulty_instructions.轻松: Lax security, bribable NPCs, slow heat gain; suited for small street jobs and newcomer sessions.
difficulty_instructions.标准: Tight corporate security, planning and trade-offs needed; failed hacks, shootouts, and betrayal visibly raise heat.
difficulty_instructions.硬核: Corporate manhunts everywhere, proactive ICE counterattacks, pricey cyberware upkeep; every violent act or failure can bring long-term debt, humanity loss, or a manhunt.
